stem: A 60-foot building stands on level ground. From a point on the ground, the angle of elevation to the top of the building is 53 degrees. Given that sin 53° ≈ 4/5, cos 53° ≈ 3/5, and tan 53° ≈ 4/3, what is the straight-line distance from the point on the ground to the top of the building, in feet?
explanation: In this right triangle, the building height (60 feet) is the side opposite the 53° angle and the straight-line distance from the ground point to the top is the hypotenuse. Using sin 53° = opposite/hypotenuse: 4/5 = 60/hypotenuse, so hypotenuse = 60 ÷ (4/5) = 60 × (5/4) = 300/4 = 75 feet.

distractorRationale.A: Used tan instead of sin — divided building height by tan 53° instead of sin 53°: 60 ÷ (4/3) = 60 × (3/4) = 45
distractorRationale.B: Set up the ratio inverted — multiplied by sin instead of dividing, computing height × sin 53° instead of height ÷ sin 53°: 60 × (4/5) = 48
distractorRationale.D: Used cos instead of sin — divided building height by cos 53° instead of sin 53°: 60 ÷ (3/5) = 60 × (5/3) = 100
